display.purpose: Monumental Syne 800 statements: hero line, section titles, Nullfast card name, project title, contact title.

title.purpose: Mid-level Syne 700 headings: trajectory names, case-part h3, pull-quote.

body.purpose: DM Sans Light 300 ledes and paragraphs; measure capped 54-62ch.

label.purpose: DM Mono uppercase inscription voice: rail field names, decision keys, screenshot captions. Almost always Gilt.

mono.purpose: DM Mono normal-case annotation voice: nav links, meta, stack table, status tablets, CTA links, footer.

shadows.name: hearth-glow
shadows.purpose: The self-emitting glow of the Ember oculus at the rosette center. The ONLY shadow in the system; it is emitted light, not shade. Breathes to 0 0 10px 1px 28% via ember-breathe (4.5s).

motion.name: ease-signature
motion.purpose: The single governing easing for reveals, hovers, underline wipes, arrow translations, and view transitions.

motion.name: ease-fade
motion.purpose: Plain fade-in for hero stamp, spec fields, and rosette entrance.

motion.name: dur-reveal
motion.purpose: Reveal-on-scroll opacity/transform duration (.7s for staggered children).

motion.name: ember-breathe
motion.purpose: The hearth oculus opacity + glow pulse; the system's only looping animation.

breakpoints.name: fold
breakpoints.purpose: The single breakpoint. Below it the spec rail folds inline, desktop nav becomes a hamburger sheet, and multi-column grids collapse.

components.name: Status Tablet
components.description: Inscribed, non-interactive state marker (Live on Play / In development). Carved plate: live corners, Ember stroke.
components.css: .ds-status-tablet { display: inline-block; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #C84A18; background: transparent; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #C84A18 35%, transparent); border-radius: 0; padding: 4px 9px; }

components.name: CTA Link
components.description: The system's only action affordance: a mono text link in Ember with an underline that follows to Ember Bright on hover. No filled buttons exist.
components.html: View on Play Store
components.css: .ds-cta-link { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #C84A18; border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #C84A18 40%, transparent); padding-bottom: 3px; text-decoration: none; transition: color .15s, border-color .15s; } .ds-cta-link:hover { color: #E5622E; border-color: #E5622E; } .ds-cta-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E5622E; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Nav Toggle
components.description: Incidental control (language/theme). The one place a soft 6px corner is permitted.
components.css: .ds-nav-toggle { font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #807D78; background: transparent; border: 1px solid rgba(245,244,240,0.12); border-radius: 6px; padding: 6px 9px; cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; transition: color .15s, border-color .15s; } .ds-nav-toggle:hover { color: #F5F4F0; border-color: #807D78; } .ds-nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E5622E; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Top-nav link with an Ember underline that wipes in left-to-right on hover as text lifts from Ash-Mute to Ash.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { position: relative; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #807D78; padding: 8px 11px; text-decoration: none; transition: color .2s ease; } .ds-nav-link::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 11px; right: 11px; bottom: 5px; height: 1px; background: #C84A18; transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left center; transition: transform .35s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #F5F4F0; } .ds-nav-link:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); }

components.name: Case Card
components.description: Rule-defined case card: no fill, no shadow, radius 0. Bottom border and name shift on hover — tone moves, nothing lifts.
components.html:
Nullfast

A timer that starts fast, a history that outlives the phone, and nothing else.

Read the full case →
components.css: .ds-case-card { display: block; background: transparent; border: none; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(245,244,240,0.12); border-radius: 0; padding: 8px 0 44px; transition: border-color .3s ease; } .ds-case-card:hover { border-color: #4A4744; } .ds-case-name { display: block; font-family: 'Syne', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 64px; letter-spacing: -0.045em; line-height: 0.92; color: #F5F4F0; margin-bottom: 26px; transition: color .25s ease; } .ds-case-card:hover .ds-case-name { color: #E5622E; } .ds-case-sum { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 300; font-size: 17px; line-height: 1.6; color: #BFBCB6; max-width: 54ch; margin-bottom: 34px; } .ds-case-cta { font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #C84A18; text-decoration: none; }

components.name: Rail Label
components.description: The signature margin-rail identity: Gilt uppercase field, mono meta, a 1px vertical rule, and an Ember diamond changelog node with a horizontal capital.
components.html: Method4 decisions · one stack
components.css: .ds-rail { position: relative; border-right: 1px solid rgba(245,244,240,0.12); padding: 4px 48px 0 0; text-align: right; min-height: 60px; } .ds-rail::after { content: ''; position: absolute; right: -4.5px; top: 6px; width: 8px; height: 8px; background: #C84A18; transform: rotate(45deg); } .ds-rail::before { content: ''; position: absolute; right: -11px; top: -1px; width: 22px; height: 1px; background: rgba(245,244,240,0.12); } .ds-rail-field { display: block; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11.5px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #A9854D; } .ds-rail-meta { display: block; margin-top: 8px; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; line-height: 1.75; color: #4A4744; }

components.name: Rosette
components.description: The signature CSS-only rose window with a breathing Ember hearth oculus. The emotional center of the identity; pure gradients and masks, no image.
components.css: .ds-rose { position: relative; width: 110px; height: 110px; border: 1px solid rgba(245,244,240,0.12); border-radius: 50%; background: radial-gradient(circle closest-side, transparent 0 86%, rgba(245,244,240,0.12) 87% 88%, transparent 89%); } .ds-rose::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; border-radius: 50%; background: repeating-conic-gradient(rgba(245,244,240,0.12) 0 1.3deg, transparent 1.3deg 22.5deg); -webkit-mask: radial-gradient(circle closest-side, transparent 0 61%, #000 62%); mask: radial-gradient(circle closest-side, transparent 0 61%, #000 62%); } .ds-rose::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 19px; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #A9854D 70%, transparent); border-radius: 50%; background: repeating-conic-gradient(from 22.5deg, rgba(245,244,240,0.07) 0 2deg, transparent 2deg 45deg); -webkit-mask: radial-gradient(circle closest-side, transparent 0 50%, #000 51%); mask: radial-gradient(circle closest-side, transparent 0 50%, #000 51%); } .ds-rose > span { position: absolute; left: 50%; top: 50%; width: 9px; height: 9px; margin: -4.5px 0 0 -4.5px; background: #C84A18; transform: rotate(45deg); animation: ds-ember-breathe 4.5s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-ember-breathe { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; box-shadow: 0 0 20px 3px color-mix(in oklab, #C84A18 55%, transparent); } 50% { opacity: .65; box-shadow: 0 0 10px 1px color-mix(in oklab, #C84A18 28%, transparent); } }

narrative.overview: velkyss.dev reads like an engineering specification that has been carved into stone. Two disciplines are fused: the rigor of a technical document (a changelog rail down the left margin, monospace field labels, roman-numeral part markers, spec-stamp headers) and the permanence of sacred architecture (a rosette window, Byzantine gilt inscriptions, tessera dot-fields, Khmer corbel thresholds). The thesis of the portfolio ('judgment lives in what I decide not to build') is expressed materially: nothing is elevated or decorated for effect; every mark is inscribed, justified, load-bearing. The system is dark by brand default (warm near-black basalt), monumentally typeset in Syne, and annotated everywhere in DM Mono. Color is almost absent, so the one warm color, Ember, lands as an event: it is the color of decision and appears only where a decision was made. Motion is ritual, not decorative: a single cubic-bezier governs almost everything, content reveals once and holds still, and the only self-emitting light is the slow ember-breathe glow of the hearth oculus.
keyCharacteristics: Spec-document scaffolding (margin rail, mono labels, roman numerals, field stamps) as the primary structure.
Sacred-architecture ornament (rosette, gilt, tesserae, corbels) rendered in pure CSS, never as images.
Flat by doctrine: depth is tonal and hairline, never shadow.
Ember as a rationed event color — decision made visible.
Warm neutrals (basalt/ash), never pure black or gray.
Live corners (radius-0) on anything carved; small radii only on incidental controls.

rules.name: The Decision Rule
rules.body: Ember means a decision was made. It appears only on choices (the criterio underline, rail nodes, active status, CTAs) and never as a background fill or large area. Its rarity is the argument.

rules.name: The Two Metals Rule
rules.body: Gilt inscribes, Ember decides. Gilt is never interactive and Ember is never a passive label. If an element catalogues it is Gilt; if it asserts a choice or action it is Ember. They never trade jobs.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Rule
rules.body: No pure black, white, or neutral gray. Every neutral carries warmth (basalt/ash); the stone is never cold.

rules.name: The Annotation Rule
rules.body: Anything that is metadata, a label, a status, a key, a caption, or a call-to-action is DM Mono. Prose and headings are never mono; annotations are never Syne or DM Sans. The mono/serif split is the spec metaphor.

rules.name: The Light-Body Rule
rules.body: Body copy is DM Sans Light (300). Weight is added only to pull a single term to Ash (500); emphasis is by tone and weight, never italic or color alone.

rules.name: The Rail Rule
rules.body: The 164px margin-rail is the signature. New sections use the two-column grid and put their identity (Gilt label + mono meta + Ember node) in the rail, never inline with the body.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: UI elements cast no shadow. If something needs to feel distinct, change its tonal surface (basalt step) or add a hairline. The single glow is reserved for the Ember hearth.

rules.name: The Carved-Corner Rule
rules.body: Content artifacts (tablets, cards, frames, bands) have radius 0. Only throwaway controls get a small radius. Radius signals 'incidental UI'; its absence signals 'part of the record'.

dos: Do put every section's identity (Gilt label + mono meta + Ember diamond node) in the 164px rail; let it fold to an inline row below 900px.
Do ration Ember to decisions and actions only, kept well under ~10% of any screen.
Do keep Gilt strictly to inscriptions (labels, numerals, keys, captions) and never make it clickable.
Do render ornament (rosettes, tesserae, corbels) in pure CSS gradients/masks, never as images.
Do use radius 0 for anything carved; reserve 6px only for incidental controls.
Do convey depth with tonal basalt steps and 1px hairlines; keep the single cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) easing.
Do use tokens (--accent, --gilt, --ink*, --fog*), never raw hex, and keep both light and dark themes working through the variable remap.
donts: Don't introduce a filled/pill button, a gradient CTA, or a rounded-everything control — the portfolio's whole point is refusal of that pattern.
Don't add drop shadows or card elevation; the only glow belongs to the Ember hearth.
Don't use pure black, pure white, or neutral gray — every neutral is warm (basalt/ash).
Don't set labels/status/CTAs in Syne or DM Sans, or set prose/headings in DM Mono — the mono/serif split is the spec metaphor.
Don't let Ember and Gilt trade jobs (Gilt is never action, Ember is never a passive label).
Don't inflate: present in-development apps as in-development, and cite only real, dated metrics.
